title: Audited Financial Statements — Qdoba Funding Holdco LLC and Subsidiaries
why_it_matters: Franchisor's indirect parent entity that guarantees franchise obligations. Balance sheets, income statements, member's equity, and cash flows. Deloitte & Touche LLP audited, clean opinion. Pages use CID-encoded fonts that resist standard text extraction.
key_burden_or_function: Guaranty of performance from Qdoba Funding Holdco LLC for franchisor's duties under Franchise Agreement. Entity formed September 7, 2023 as part of securitization; cannot yet provide three full years of audited statements.

title: Audited Financial Statements — Qdoba Corporation and Subsidiaries
why_it_matters: Qdoba Corporation is the indirect parent that owns QRC (the management services provider). Fully readable financial statements: balance sheets, operations statements, stockholder's equity, cash flows. Deloitte clean opinion. Revenue $383M, net loss $9.2M (FY2025). Also includes guaranty of QRC management agreement obligations.
key_burden_or_function: Qdoba Corp guarantees QRC's duties under the management agreement with the franchisor. These statements are for disclosure purposes only — Qdoba Corp is not party to the Franchise Agreement.

title: List of State Administrators
why_it_matters: Contact information for state franchise regulatory agencies.
key_burden_or_function: Reference only; no burden on franchisee.

title: Agents for Service of Process
why_it_matters: State-by-state agents for service of process.

title: List of Current Franchised Locations and Former Franchisees
why_it_matters: 33-page exhibit containing: (1) current franchised locations with entity name, address, phone (pages 132-160); (2) former franchisees — store transfers franchise-to-company (8 stores across NJ, FL), franchise-to-franchisee (11 transfers in NC, PA, CA), store closures (9 closures in FY2025, pages 161-162); (3) signed-but-not-opened agreements (14 locations, page 163). Critical for diligence contact and concentration analysis.
key_burden_or_function: Due diligence reference for prospective franchisees. Confidentiality clauses noted — some franchisees restricted from speaking openly about their experience.

title: Franchise Agreement
why_it_matters: 74-page standard Franchise Agreement for traditional Qdoba restaurants. Contains all material terms governing the franchise relationship: 10-year term, fee obligations, operating standards, territory protections, non-compete, transfer restrictions, termination rights, dispute resolution. Includes attached exhibits: Schedule A (site details), Exhibit A (site acceptance), Exhibit B (Guaranty), Exhibit C (Confidentiality/Non-Compete), Exhibit D (Non-Disclosure), Exhibit E (Conditional Assignment of Lease).
key_burden_or_function: Defines all franchise obligations. Key burden provisions: royalty 5%, marketing 4.5%, technology fees, personal guaranty required, non-compete 2 years/5 miles post-term, California arbitration, first-priority security interest in franchise assets, ACH prearranged payment authorization.

title: License Agreement
why_it_matters: 34-page License Agreement for non-traditional locations (military bases, universities, airports, etc.). Reduced fee structure vs traditional FA: franchise fee $20,000, royalty 3%, marketing 1.75%.
key_burden_or_function: Governs non-traditional franchise operations. Lower fees but similar operational requirements. No territory protection typically provided.

title: Development Agreement
why_it_matters: 47-page Development Agreement for multi-unit development rights. Defines Development Area, minimum development quotas, development schedule, territorial protections during DA term. Includes attached exhibits: Schedule A (development details), Exhibit A (development schedule), Exhibits B-E (guaranty, confidentiality, non-disclosure, assignment).
key_burden_or_function: Grants right to develop 1+ restaurants in defined area. $10,000 development fee per site. Failure to meet development schedule = loss of fee credit, material default, potential immediate termination of DA and all territorial protections.

title: Table of Contents — Manuals
why_it_matters: 14-page table of contents for the confidential operating manuals. Reveals scope of operational control: recipes, preparation procedures, equipment specifications, quality standards, marketing guidelines, technology requirements.
key_burden_or_function: Franchisee must comply with all manual requirements. Manuals maintained primarily in electronic format and may be modified at franchisor's discretion.

title: State-Specific Addenda and Agreement Amendments
why_it_matters: 77-page exhibit covering state-specific modifications for 11 states: California, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, New York, North Dakota, Rhode Island, Virginia, Washington. These addenda may materially modify termination rights, renewal rights, choice of law, dispute resolution, and other provisions.
key_burden_or_function: State laws may override certain FA terms. Critical for franchisees in regulated states. Addenda exist for both the FDD and the individual agreements (FA, LA, DA).

title: Certification of Entity Structure and Operation Form
why_it_matters: Form requiring franchisee to certify its entity structure, ownership, and operational arrangements.

title: General Release of All Claims
why_it_matters: 3-page general release form used at renewal, transfer, and other events. Franchisee releases all claims against Qdoba. Note: release does not waive claims under applicable state franchise relationship laws that prohibit such waivers.
key_burden_or_function: Required at renewal and transfer. Releases all claims. Some states prohibit or limit such releases.

title: Master Technology Agreement
why_it_matters: 8-page technology agreement defining IT infrastructure requirements and fees. Technology fee: $250/restaurant/period + 0.21% of Gross Sales. Covers POS, credit card processing, inventory management, online ordering, loyalty system, kitchen display. Qdoba retains right to modify systems and fees with 90 days notice.
key_burden_or_function: Mandatory technology stack and ongoing fees. Franchisee responsible for purchasing/maintaining hardware (POS terminals, firewall, cabling, internet). Qdoba has right to access franchisee's POS data and modify technology requirements unilaterally.

title: Authorization for Prearranged Payments
why_it_matters: ACH debit authorization allowing Qdoba to withdraw fees directly from franchisee bank accounts. Standard requirement for all franchisees.
key_burden_or_function: Franchisor has direct access to franchisee's bank account for royalty, marketing, technology, and other fee collections.

title: Non-Disclosure Agreement
why_it_matters: 3-page NDA for use with prospective franchisees during the evaluation process.

title: Stored Value Card Service Agreement
why_it_matters: 6-page agreement with QMG Stored Value Cards, LLC (affiliate) for the Q-card gift/stored value card program. Mandatory participation in the loyalty/gift card program.
key_burden_or_function: Franchisee must participate in the stored value card program. Payments processed through affiliate QMG Stored Value Cards, LLC.
